description: This figure presents a comprehensive scatter plot summarizing the room-temperature tensile properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels with varying manganese contents. The x-axis shows yield/tensile strength in MPa (200–1600 MPa), while the y-axis indicates total elongation in percentage (0–100%). Open symbols represent yield strength and filled symbols denote tensile strength. Data points are color- and shape-coded by Mn content: red triangles (12–18 wt.%), magenta squares (18–25 wt.%), green triangles (25–28 wt.%), and orange hexagons (28–33 wt.%). Dashed ellipses demarcate five microstructural categories: single austenite phase, single ferrite phase, ferrite-austenite-MC/κ, austenite-based duplex, and ferrite-based duplex. Three dashed contour lines mark strength-ductility product levels of 30, 50, and 70 GPa·%. The figure reveals that single austenite and austenite-based duplex steels generally occupy the upper-right region, exhibiting superior strength-ductility balance. Higher Mn contents expand the accessible property space toward both higher strength and elongation, and the overall distribution follows a banana-shaped curve that illustrates the trade-off and optimization potential between strength and ductility.
